# This file represents stdout captured from a CLI that prints help text
# but exits with a non-zero status code (e.g., exit code 1).
#
# Exit behavior: CLI exits 1 even when --help is passed.
# discover.py should: parse this output anyway, and add a warning entry
# to discovery.warnings (e.g., "CLI exited with code 1 during help fetch").
# The discovery run should NOT abort on non-zero exit when stdout is non-empty.
#
# Real-world example: some CLIs (older versions of git subcommands,
# certain Docker plugins) return exit 1 on --help while printing full help.

BADCLI — A CLI that exits non-zero on --help.

USAGE
  badcli <command> [flags]

COMMANDS
  start:    Start the process
  stop:     Stop the process
  status:   Show process status
  config:   Manage configuration

FLAGS
  --help      Show help for command
  --version   Show version

EXAMPLES
  $ badcli start
  $ badcli status

Note: This CLI always exits 1, even when displaying help.
